briefdescription.para: Max page size of NAND flash.
Max spare bytes of a NAND flash page.
Page is not valid in command phase.
Column is not valid in command phase.
AXI Data width for last transaction while reading and writing.
ECC error correction byte position mask, bits[11:3] of error code.
ECC error correction bit position mask, bits[0:2] of error code.
OneHot is used to check if one and only one bit is set.

detaileddescription.para: This Macro returns 1 if the value passed is OneHot.
The user is required to allocate a variable of this type for every flash device in the system. A pointer to a variable of this type is then passed to the driver API functions.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XNandPs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xnandps.h for the definition of XNandPs_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a specific XNandPs device/instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the flash device to read or write any data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
points to the XNandPs device configuration structure.
is the base address of SMC controller.
is the base address of NAND flash.

simplesect.para: The user needs to first call the XNandPs_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer which is passed as a parameter to the XNandPs_CfgInitialize() API.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPs *InstancePtr, u64 Offset, u32 Length, void *DestPtr, u8 *UserSparePtr)
memberdef.name: XNandPs_Read

briefdescription.para: This function reads the data from the Flash device and copies it into the specified user buffer.

detaileddescription.para: It doesn't check for the bad blocks while reading the flash pages that cross block boundary. User must take care of handling bad blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to read from.
is number of bytes to read.
is the destination address to copy data to.
is the user buffer to which spare data must be copied.

simplesect.para: This function reads sequential pages from the Flash device.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPs *InstancePtr, u64 Offset, u32 Length, void *DestPtr, u8 *UserSparePtr)
memberdef.name: XNandPs_ReadCache

briefdescription.para: This function reads the data from the Flash device using read page cache command and copies it into the specified user buffer.

detaileddescription.para: It doesn't check for the bad blocks while reading the flash pages that cross block boundary. User must take care of handling bad blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to read from.
is number of bytes to read.
is the destination address to copy data to.
is the user buffer to which spare data must be copied.

simplesect.para: This function reads sequential pages from the Flash device.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPs *InstancePtr, u64 Offset, u32 Length, void *SrcPtr, u8 *UserSparePtr)
memberdef.name: XNandPs_Write

briefdescription.para: This function programs the flash device(s) with data specified in the user buffer.

detaileddescription.para: The source and destination address must be aligned to the width of the flash's data bus. It doesn't check for the bad blocks while writing to the flash pages that cross block boundary. User must take care of handling bad blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to write to.
is number of bytes to write.
is the source address to write the data from.
is the user buffer which contains buffer to write into spare data area.

listitem.para: XST_NAND_WRITE_PROTECTED if the flash is write protected.

simplesect.para: This function writes number of sequential pages into the Flash device.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPs *InstancePtr, u64 Offset, u32 Length, void *SrcPtr, u8 *UserSparePtr)
memberdef.name: XNandPs_WriteCache

briefdescription.para: This function programs the flash device(s) with data specified in the user buffer using program cache command.

detaileddescription.para: The source and destination address must be aligned to the width of the flash's data bus. It doesn't check for the bad blocks while writing to the flash pages that cross block boundary. User must take care of handling bad blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to write to.
is number of bytes to write.
is the source address to write the data from.
is the user buffer which contains buffer to write into spare data area.

listitem.para: XST_NAND_WRITE_PROTECTED if the flash is write protected.

simplesect.para: This function writes number of sequential pages into the Flash device.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the spare area of a page.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the page number from where spare data is read.
is pointer to the buffer where the spare data is filled.

briefdescription.para: This function write to the spare area of a page.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the page number to write.
is pointer to the buffer which holds the data.

briefdescription.para: This function erases a specific block in the NAND device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the block number of the device.

listitem.para: XST_NAND_WRITE_PROTECTED if the flash is write protected.
